heading: Imported distilled spirits
label: 5232 Imported distilled spirits
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 5232, acts , ; , , , related to bond requirements of internal revenue bonded warehouses, prior to the general revision of this chapter by . See sections 5005(c)(1), 5006(a)(2), 5173(a), (c)(1), 5174(a)(1), 5176(a), (b), and 5177(b)(1) of this title.Aug. 16, 1954, ch. 73668A Stat. 643Pub. L. 85–859, title II, § 206(b)72 Stat. 1431Pub. L. 85–859Sept. 2, 1958Provisions similar to those comprising this section were contained in prior section 5311, , , prior to the general revision of this chapter by .act Aug. 16, 1954, ch. 73668A Stat. 658Pub. L. 85–859

Amendments1979—Subsec. (b). redesignated par. (2) and (3) as (1) and (2). Former par. (1), which prohibited distilled spirits transferred pursuant to subsection (a) from being bottled in bond under section 5233, was struck out.Pub. L. 96–391976—Subsec. (a). inserted “, or the person bringing such distilled spirits into the United States,” after “and the importer”.Pub. L. 94–4551971—Subsec. (a). , extended privilege of transfer of distilled spirits to the plant without payment of tax to distilled spirits imported, or brought into the United States, and struck out reference to section 5001.Pub. L. 91–659, § 7(a)Subsec. (b). , struck out “Imported” before “distilled spirits” and thus applied subsection to all distilled spirits.Pub. L. 91–659, § 7(b)1968— permitted withdrawal in bulk containers or by pipeline from customs custody to internal revenue bond without payment of internal revenue taxes of all imported distilled spirits in bulk containers, regardless of proof, extended to all such imported distilled spirits the withdrawal privileges already available to imported distilled spirits of at least 185 proof, whether or not they have been redistilled or denatured, provided that transferor’s liability for the internal revenue tax ceases when the transferee’s liability attaches, and established that imported bulk spirits are not eligible for the bottled in bond privileges available to domestic spirits.Pub. L. 90–630

children.heading: Transfer to distilled spirits plant without payment of tax
children.label: a Transfer to distilled spirits plant without payment of tax
children.text: Distilled spirits imported or brought into the United States in bulk containers may, under such regulations as the Secretary shall prescribe, be withdrawn from customs custody and transferred in such bulk containers or by pipeline to the bonded premises of a distilled spirits plant without payment of the internal revenue tax imposed on such distilled spirits. The person operating the bonded premises of the distilled spirits plant to which such spirits are transferred shall become liable for the tax on distilled spirits withdrawn from customs custody under this section upon release of the spirits from customs custody, and the importer, or the person bringing such distilled spirits into the United States, shall thereupon be relieved of his liability for such tax.
may be redistilled or denatured only if of 185 degrees or more of proof, and
may be withdrawn for any purpose authorized by this chapter, in the same manner as domestic distilled spirits.
